families.name: Chemical formulae and balanced equations
families.route: formula/equation interpretation → atom balance → coefficient validation
families.notes: Foundation for all stoichiometric calculations; include state symbols and ionic equations where relevant.

families.name: Relative atomic mass from isotope abundance
families.route: weighted abundance → weighted sum → divide by total abundance
families.notes: Support percentage and relative-abundance forms.

families.name: Relative formula mass and molar mass
families.route: parse formula → count atoms → sum Ar values
families.notes: Must distinguish atoms, molecules, formula units and hydrated salts.

families.name: Mole, Avogadro constant and particle count
families.route: identify entity → convert amount ↔ particles using L
families.notes: Entity identity is part of the answer contract.

families.name: Empirical formula
families.route: mass/% → moles → divide by smallest → integer ratio
families.notes: Include hydration and combustion variants later.

families.name: Molecular formula
families.route: empirical formula mass → molecular mass ratio → multiply subscripts
families.notes: Requires reliable Mr evidence.

families.name: Single-step reacting mass
families.route: given mass → amount → coefficient ratio → target amount → target mass
families.notes: Current MgO component is a smoke test for this family.

families.name: Multi-equation reaction chain
families.route: identify stage graph → propagate mole ratios across equations → target
families.notes: Core product narrative; requires strategy selection, not a fixed one-step template.

families.name: Percentage composition and percentage purity
families.route: measured pure amount → mass of target component → divide by sample mass × 100
families.bookRef: Ch.2 percentage composition; Ch.3 impure limestone example
families.notes: Treat purity as a separate decision node from yield.

families.name: Percentage yield
families.route: limiting input → theoretical yield → actual/theoretical × 100
families.notes: Must separate density conversion, limiting reagent and yield.

families.name: Limiting reagent
families.route: convert each reactant to amount → normalise by coefficient → choose minimum
families.bookRef: Ch.2, worked example around printed pp.37–38
families.notes: Do not infer limiting reagent from smaller mass or volume.

families.name: Excess reagent and amount remaining
families.route: find limiting reagent → calculate amount consumed → subtract from initial excess
families.notes: Needs explicit consumed-vs-initial bookkeeping.

families.name: Deduce stoichiometric relationships
families.route: derive mole relationships from measured masses/volumes/concentrations
families.notes: Often inverse problems; may require symbolic reasoning.

families.name: Gas-volume stoichiometry
families.route: same T/P → coefficient ratio equals gas-volume ratio
families.notes: Track physical state; liquid water is not part of a gas-volume ratio.

families.name: Molar gas volume
families.route: volume ↔ amount using stated molar volume
families.notes: Never hard-code 24 dm³ mol⁻¹ when the question supplies another condition.

families.name: Ideal gas equation
families.route: normalise units → choose unknown → rearrange pV=nRT → evaluate
families.notes: Unit conversion is a first-class diagnostic node.

families.name: Gas data to Mr or composition
families.route: pVT → amount → molar mass or formula
families.notes: Can combine purity and gas collection.

families.name: Mass concentration and amount concentration
families.route: convert mass ↔ amount; divide/multiply by solution volume
families.notes: Volume conversion cm³ ↔ dm³ is a common first error.

families.name: Solution stoichiometry
families.route: cV → amount → ratio → target amount/concentration/mass
families.notes: General substrate for titration.

families.name: Acid–base titration calculations
families.route: select concordant titre → mean → cV → ratio → unknown
families.notes: Separate practical data selection from stoichiometric calculation.

families.name: Redox titration calculations
families.route: balanced redox equation → cV → electron/mole ratio → unknown
families.bookRef: Ch.4 titration examples; Ch.1 redox equations
families.notes: Include manganate(VII)/Fe²⁺, manganate(VII)/oxalate and Cu²⁺/I⁻.

families.name: Iodine–thiosulfate titration
families.route: identify iodine-generating stage → thiosulfate ratio → back-calculate analyte
families.notes: Usually multi-stage and should exercise route planning.

families.name: Calorimetry q=mcΔT and molar ΔH
families.route: temperature change → q → divide by reacting amount → sign/units
families.notes: Heat-loss and limiting-amount assumptions need explicit evidence.

families.name: Bond-energy enthalpy
families.route: sum bonds broken − sum bonds formed
families.notes: Count bonds from structures; distinguish exact and average values.

families.name: Hess-law cycles
families.route: construct cycle → orient equations → algebraic sum
families.notes: Multiple valid cycle representations should be accepted.

families.name: Born–Haber cycles
families.route: atomisation/ionisation/electron affinity/lattice cycle → solve unknown
families.notes: Must enforce the syllabus ion-charge limits.

families.name: Hydration and solution enthalpy cycles
families.route: lattice + hydration ↔ solution cycle
families.notes: Check sign convention carefully.

families.name: Entropy change
families.route: ΣS°products − ΣS°reactants
families.notes: Keep J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹ until conversion for Gibbs.

families.name: Gibbs free energy and feasibility
families.route: unit alignment → ΔG=ΔH−TΔS → sign/threshold temperature
families.notes: Separate standard feasibility from kinetic rate.

families.name: Kc and Kp expressions/calculations
families.route: derive expression → equilibrium quantities → substitute → units
families.notes: No quadratic equations required by syllabus.

families.name: Strong acid/base pH
families.route: concentration → [H⁺]/[OH⁻] → pH/pOH as required
families.notes: Include dilution and mixtures only when evidence is complete.

families.name: Weak acid pH and Ka
families.route: Ka expression → justified approximation/solve → pH
families.notes: Schema should record approximation assumptions.

families.name: Buffer pH
families.route: identify conjugate pair → amounts after reaction → Ka ratio → pH
families.notes: Support buffers formed by partial neutralisation.

families.name: Ksp and common-ion calculations
families.route: write Ksp → stoichiometric concentration relationship → solve/compare
families.notes: Distinguish solubility from concentration of each ion.

families.name: Partition coefficient
families.route: equilibrium concentration ratio → mass balance → extraction result
families.notes: Only same physical state in both solvents is required.

families.name: Rate from data or graph
families.route: identify variable → Δquantity/Δtime or tangent gradient
families.notes: Graph reading provenance is essential.

families.name: Orders and rate equations
families.route: compare experiments/graphs → deduce orders → construct rate equation
families.notes: Accept initial-rate and half-life methods.

families.name: Rate constant and first-order half-life
families.route: rate equation or k=0.693/t½ → calculate k and units
families.notes: Dimensional consistency is part of validation.

families.name: Electrolysis: Q=It and Faraday calculations
families.route: Q=It → mol e⁻=Q/F → electron ratio → mass/volume
families.notes: Requires original school-authored note and cases.

families.name: Standard cell potential
families.route: select half-cells → E°cathode−E°anode → direction/feasibility
families.notes: Equation construction and sign convention must be separate checks.

families.name: Nernst equation
families.route: identify oxidised/reduced concentrations → z → substitute in Nernst equation
families.notes: Current-syllabus supplement required.

families.name: ΔG° = −nE°cellF
families.route: balanced redox electrons → E°cell → ΔG° with units/sign
families.notes: Current-syllabus supplement required.

families.name: Transition-metal redox stoichiometry
families.route: construct/use redox equation → amount ratio → target
families.notes: Use current 9701 systems as governed cases.

families.name: Stability constant Kstab
families.route: write formation expression → substitute concentrations → solve
families.notes: Current-syllabus supplement required.

families.name: Mass-spectrum isotope calculations
families.route: m/e and abundance → isotope identification → weighted Ar
families.notes: Charts/images need structured evidence extraction.

families.name: [M+1]+ carbon-count calculation
families.route: 100×abundance(M+1)/(1.1×abundance(M))
families.bookRef: Mass spectrometry chapter does not clearly target current formula
families.notes: Add current-syllabus note and cases.

families.name: Chromatogram percentage composition
families.route: peak area/total area × 100
families.notes: Image/graph extraction may be needed.

families.name: Units, significant figures and rounding
families.route: track source precision → unit consistency → justified rounding
families.notes: Must be reusable across every calculation component.

families.name: Uncertainty and percentage error
families.route: instrument resolution → absolute uncertainty → propagated/percentage error
families.notes: Needs 9701-specific teacher notes.

families.name: Concordant titres and mean selection
families.route: exclude rough/anomalous → identify concordant set → mean
families.notes: Selection evidence should be explicit in trace.

families.name: Graph gradient, intercept, tangent and best fit
families.route: choose axes/scale → fit → select points → calculate/interpret
families.notes: Requires image-aware evidence or structured graph data.

families.name: Gravimetric composition and hydration
families.route: mass change → amount lost/remaining → ratio/formula/purity
families.notes: Heating to constant mass is part of practical evidence.

families.name: Thermometric experimental calculations
families.route: select ΔT → q → amount → ΔH → evaluate loss
families.notes: Includes data quality and evaluation.

families.name: Gas-collection composition/purity
families.route: gas measurement → amount → stoichiometry → composition/purity
families.bookRef: Ch.3, impure limestone and gas examples
families.notes: Strong pilot family for Mg + acid / H₂ purity cases.
